hook: Software that pays an EV fleet's electric bill 30% lower without buying a single charger.
core_problem: 120k US commercial EV fleets pay 2-4x retail kWh because they charge at peak; charging-network apps don't see fleet TMS data.
ai_leverage: Schedule agent reads dispatch + telematics; charging agent stages sessions during off-peak windows + utility DR programs.
monetization_logic: 30% of measured kWh-cost savings; minimum $500/mo per fleet.
distribution_strategy: Fleet-mgmt SaaS partnerships (Geotab, Samsara) + utility partnerships for DR program enrollment.
retention_loop: Per-fleet charging history + utility tariffs lock optimization accuracy; switching loses months of tuning.

moat_potential.rationale: Per-fleet + per-utility charging-cost corpus improves recommendations.

scalability_rationale: Asset-light; SaaS channel scales fast; utility DR partnerships gate territory.
billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: small commercial fleets. Stage 2: school + transit fleets. Stage 3: V2G arbitrage product.
failure_avoidance: hardware_scaling_chasm: software-only over Canoo's hardware burn
commoditization_collapse: own optimization, not the chargers
validation_invocation: Own the per-fleet + per-utility optimization data; charger OEMs see only their own hardware.

technical_mvp_sketch.data: Telematics, dispatch, utility tariffs, DR signals.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: Operator approves new tariff or new utility partnership.

ui_ux_invocation: Fleet manager sees a monthly savings report; daily ops are unchanged.

hook: A vision-AI inspection that buyers trust more than a CarFax — and dealers can run from a phone.
core_problem: 39M used cars sold/yr in US; dealers + private sellers lack trustworthy condition data; CarFax misses 60% of body damage.
ai_leverage: Vision agent runs a 60-second guided phone scan; defect agent classifies frame, paint, undercarriage, mechanical signals.
distribution_strategy: Dealer-management-system partnerships (vAuto, CDK) + D2C via Craigslist/Facebook Marketplace integration.
retention_loop: Per-VIN inspection history compounds; switching loses verified-condition badge.

moat_potential.rationale: Per-VIN inspection corpus is unique; competitors must rebuild from zero.

billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: D2C + small dealer. Stage 2: enterprise dealer + auction house. Stage 3: insurance + financing pricing layer.
failure_avoidance: thin_wrapper_syndrome: own the defect classifier, not a UI on third-party CV
unsustainable_unit_economics: asset-light vs Vroom
validation_invocation: Defensible per-VIN data + dealer workflow lock; reject vanity inspection counts.

technical_mvp_sketch.stack: React Native + Python + Postgres + custom CV models
technical_mvp_sketch.data: Per-VIN inspection corpus, defect taxonomy, historical resale prices.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: Trained inspector reviews any 'high concern' flag before report ships.

ui_ux_invocation: Seller follows on-screen prompts; agent generates a buyer-shareable report in 5 minutes.

hook: An ML safety layer that drops e-bike injuries 40% by intervening before the swerve.
core_problem: 150M e-bike + scooter trips annually result in 250k ER visits; existing fleets (Lime, Bird) lack predictive safety beyond geofencing.
ai_leverage: Edge-AI on handlebar IMU + camera; rider-state agent slows or warns before high-risk swerve.
monetization_logic: $8-15 per device/mo licensed to fleet operators + cities.
distribution_strategy: Direct to fleet operators + city procurement.
retention_loop: City-level safety data + insurance discount drives operator retention.

moat_potential.rationale: City RFP requirements increasingly mandate safety analytics; first-mover wins multi-year contracts.

scalability_rationale: Hardware partner integration is the bottleneck; software margins after.
billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: micromobility fleets. Stage 2: bike-share + delivery rider safety. Stage 3: full urban mobility safety layer.
failure_avoidance: hardware_scaling_chasm: software on partner hardware vs Canoo
regulatory_blind_spot: GDPR + CCPA-aligned rider data handling
validation_invocation: Insurance + city procurement create a defensible regulatory + operational moat.

technical_mvp_sketch.stack: Edge: TFLite + Rust; cloud: Python + Postgres + Snowflake
technical_mvp_sketch.data: Per-city ride telemetry, incident records, hospital admission correlations.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: City partner approves any intervention policy change; safety officer reviews incident escalations.

ui_ux_invocation: Rider feels a slight throttle reduction in risky moments; no app interaction.

hook: A roaming charging marketplace where private home chargers earn during the day.
core_problem: 11M US home EV chargers sit idle 80% of the day; public network coverage is uneven; drivers route to gas-station-grade DCFC and pay 3x.
ai_leverage: Routing agent matches driver origin/destination/SOC to nearest cheap home charger; trust agent rates hosts + drivers.
monetization_logic: 20% take rate on session $; $0.05/kWh insurance fee.
distribution_strategy: Charger OEM partnerships (Wallbox, ChargePoint home) + EV-driver community channels.
retention_loop: Two-sided liquidity per metro; once dense, drivers and hosts can't switch without losing earnings/availability.

moat_potential.rationale: Two-sided density compounds; new entrants face cold-start in every metro.

scalability_rationale: Software-only marketplace; capital is host-supplied chargers.
billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: home roaming. Stage 2: SMB destination charging. Stage 3: V2G + grid-services bundle.
failure_avoidance: hardware_scaling_chasm: marketplace, not OEM
regulatory_blind_spot: utility tariffs + state PUC rules per metro
validation_invocation: Indispensable infrastructure for distributed charging; both sides depend on us.

technical_mvp_sketch.data: Per-charger availability, session history, driver SOC, utility tariffs.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: Ops resolves disputes >$200; safety reviews reported equipment issues.

ui_ux_invocation: Driver enters destination; agent picks the cheapest viable charger; host gets paid automatically.

hook: A returns-logistics network that turns the $800B reverse-supply problem into per-pallet profit.
core_problem: $800B/yr in US returns burns warehouse capacity + truck miles; carriers see returns as a cost line, not a routing optimization problem.
ai_leverage: Returns-routing agent consolidates pickups + matches to backhaul lanes; resale agent values items at return time and routes to liquidation/refurb/restock.
monetization_logic: $2-8 per package + 5% of resale recovered value.
retention_loop: Per-brand returns history + lane density compound; switching loses recovery rates.

moat_potential.rationale: Lane density + per-brand resale outcomes compound; cold-start barrier.

scalability_rationale: $800B TAM; software-orchestrated; capital is partner trucks.
billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: D2C apparel returns. Stage 2: cross-category. Stage 3: returns-as-a-service for marketplaces.
failure_avoidance: unsustainable_unit_economics: per-package + resale split keeps margin healthy
market_timing_failure: returns volume is structural, not COVID-driven
validation_invocation: Lane density + recovery-rate moat compounds; reject vanity volume metrics.

technical_mvp_sketch.data: Per-brand SKU resale data, lane density, carrier capacity.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: Ops approves new lane onboarding; finance reviews any monthly settlement >$1M.

ui_ux_invocation: Brand sees recovered $ per month; consumer sees a friction-free return label.

hook: A dispatch brain that lets independent taxi operators beat Uber surge pricing in their metro.
core_problem: 180k US/EU independent taxi operators lost share to Uber/Lyft; they have local relationships but no surge-pricing intelligence.
ai_leverage: Demand-prediction agent forecasts per-cell demand 30 min ahead; pricing agent matches Uber surge dynamically.
monetization_logic: $15-40 per driver/mo; cooperative rev-share for federation members.
distribution_strategy: Taxi cooperative + association partnerships (NYC TLC, Curb).
retention_loop: Per-driver + per-cell demand history compounds; switching forfeits forecast accuracy.

moat_potential.rationale: Hyperlocal demand corpus per metro is unique.

scalability_rationale: Co-op channel slow; per-metro deployment is the bottleneck.
billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: taxi co-ops. Stage 2: independent rideshare (BlaBlaCar-style). Stage 3: global federation against duopoly platforms.
failure_avoidance: commoditization_collapse: vertical-deep with cooperatives vs Uber's mass market
regulatory_blind_spot: TLC + per-city PHV rules
validation_invocation: Indispensable infrastructure for local taxi cooperatives; we make others sovereign.

technical_mvp_sketch.data: Per-cell trip history, weather, events, competitor surge feeds.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: Co-op board approves pricing policies; ops reviews algorithmic anomalies.

ui_ux_invocation: Driver sees a heatmap + pricing recommendations; chat to ask 'where should I go in 30 min'.

hook: An RV resale concierge that values, lists, inspects, and finances — without touching the asset.
core_problem: 10M US RVs depreciate $4-8k/yr; resale is fragmented, opaque, and broker-heavy; private sellers lose 18-25% to friction.
ai_leverage: Valuation agent prices per make/model/condition; listing agent generates marketplace-ready spec sheets; financing agent qualifies buyers.
monetization_logic: 1.5% seller fee + 1% financing referral; capped at $4k.
distribution_strategy: RV park + dealer partnerships; PLG via 'free valuation' wedge.
retention_loop: Resale history per VIN compounds; future trade-ins lock continuity.

moat_potential.rationale: Per-VIN resale outcome data improves valuations.

scalability_rationale: Asset-light; market is mid-tail; community channel slow.
billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: RV resale. Stage 2: boats + powersports. Stage 3: full second-hand high-ticket asset platform.
failure_avoidance: unsustainable_unit_economics: asset-light vs Vroom; capped fee keeps trust
regulatory_blind_spot: state DMV title rules per jurisdiction
validation_invocation: Asset-light moat with per-VIN data; reject inventory exposure.

technical_mvp_sketch.data: Per-VIN history, resale prices, financing rates.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: Ops verifies title transfer; finance approves any deal >$100k.

ui_ux_invocation: Seller uploads photos + VIN; concierge runs the rest.

hook: OBD-II + AI maintenance copilot that keeps a tradesperson's truck on the road and the next job booked.
core_problem: 8M US tradespeople lose $200/day per truck downtime; existing fleet telematics priced for 50+ trucks.
ai_leverage: OBD-II + audio-vibration agent classifies wear; maintenance-scheduling agent books service to avoid revenue gaps.
distribution_strategy: Trade SaaS partnerships (Jobber, Housecall Pro) + supply-house channel (Ferguson).
retention_loop: Per-truck signature compounds; service history locked.

moat_potential.rationale: Per-truck wear corpus across thousands improves fault detection.

scalability_rationale: Hardware-light dongle; SaaS distribution scales fast.
billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: trades. Stage 2: SMB delivery fleets. Stage 3: insurance + warranty partner offering.
failure_avoidance: hardware_scaling_chasm: dongle is commodity; value is software
thin_wrapper_syndrome: own the per-truck signature corpus
validation_invocation: Own the per-truck wear data layer; competitors price on miles + age alone.

technical_mvp_sketch.stack: React Native + Python + Postgres + OBD-II BLE
technical_mvp_sketch.data: OBD-II streams, audio-vibration recordings, service history.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: Mechanic reviews any 'do not drive' flag before SMS to operator.

ui_ux_invocation: Trades app shows 'service due Tuesday'; agent books with the local shop.

hook: A city OS for the curb that prices loading zones, deliveries, and ride-hails by the minute.
core_problem: 200 US cities lose $2B+/yr to mismanaged curb space; double parking, blocked bike lanes, and lost meter revenue compound.
ai_leverage: Vision-on-pole agent classifies vehicle type + duration; pricing agent dynamically prices and bills via license plate.
monetization_logic: City contract + 8-12% of dynamic curb-revenue uplift.
distribution_strategy: Direct city procurement + smart-city consortium channels.
retention_loop: Per-city curb data + state PUC contract creates multi-year lock.

moat_potential.rationale: Multi-year city contracts + integration into permit systems create high switching costs.

scalability_rationale: City sales cycle 12-24 months; once installed, expansion within metro fast.
billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: 10 mid-size cities. Stage 2: top-50 US metros. Stage 3: global curb-as-a-service infrastructure.
failure_avoidance: regulatory_blind_spot: ALPR privacy + ADA compliance per city
unsustainable_unit_economics: outcome share keeps cities aligned
validation_invocation: Indispensable infrastructure for urban mobility; cities and operators both depend on us.

technical_mvp_sketch.stack: Edge: NVIDIA Jetson + Rust; cloud: Python + Postgres + Snowflake
technical_mvp_sketch.data: Per-block curb usage, ALPR (anonymized), city permit data.
technical_mvp_sketch.infra: AWS + Snowflake; per-city data residency + privacy review.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: City staff approve every pricing schedule change; privacy officer reviews ALPR retention.

ui_ux_invocation: Driver parks; agent tracks duration + bills via plate; receipt arrives via SMS.

hook: Vertiport operations OS — gate management, charging, FAA reporting — for the eVTOL era.
core_problem: First-wave vertiports (LA, NYC, Dubai) need ops infrastructure; current providers (Skyports, UrbanV) build airfields not software.
ai_leverage: Slot-allocation agent schedules gates by aircraft type + charge time; FAA-reporting agent files Form 7233 + ATC slots.
distribution_strategy: Direct partnership with vertiport developers + eVTOL operators (Joby, Archer).
retention_loop: FAA-integration + ops history lock multi-year contracts.

moat_potential.rationale: FAA + EASA vertiport certification cycles favor first-mover; integrations are existential.

scalability_rationale: Vertiport rollout 2026-2030 is the gating factor.
billion_dollar_path: Stage 1: pilot vertiports in 3 cities. Stage 2: top-30 metros. Stage 3: global UAM ground ops standard.
failure_avoidance: hardware_scaling_chasm: software for ops, not building airframes
regulatory_blind_spot: FAA Part 139 + EASA vertiport SC-VTOL alignment
validation_invocation: Indispensable infrastructure for the UAM ecosystem; we make operators and cities sovereign.

technical_mvp_sketch.data: Aircraft telemetry, charge state, gate availability, ATC slots.
technical_mvp_sketch.hitl: Vertiport manager approves any schedule deviation; safety officer signs incident reports.

ui_ux_invocation: Manager sees a live gate map; chat to ask 'can we accept the 14:30 slot' with constraints answered.
